meta.analysisMethod: Scanned SUMMARY.md structure + keyword/content analysis of chapter files

wellCoveredTopics: Palindromic Tree / Eertree (ch88 — overview level, ~136 lines)

missingTopics.topic: FFT / NTT (Fast Fourier Transform / Number Theoretic Transform)
missingTopics.suggestedPart: Part XVII: Advanced Techniques or new Part: Polynomial Algorithms
missingTopics.reason: Fundamental for polynomial multiplication, convolution, string matching with wildcards, counting problems. No dedicated chapter exists. Only oblique references in ch104/ch118 (false positives from 'printTree' matching). Must-have for competitive programming.
Finds minimal linear recurrence from sequence terms. Combined with matrix exponentiation, solves many counting problems. Not mentioned anywhere in the book.
Only mentioned in passing in ch47 and ch51 as a decision tree item. No implementation, no dedicated section. Important graph algorithm reducible to SCC.

missingTopics.topic: Hungarian Algorithm (Weighted Bipartite Matching / Assignment Problem)
missingTopics.reason: Only a brief overview in ch58 (~2 lines). No implementation. Classic O(n³) algorithm for weighted assignment. Should have full implementation.
Only mentioned as a reduction target in ch29 (assignment problems). No implementation of Successive Shortest Path or Cycle Canceling. ch83 covers Dinic's but not MCMF.
Not mentioned anywhere. Key tool for counting under symmetry (necklace problems, colorings). Belongs alongside combinatorics (ch71).
Only a passing mention in ch149 (one line) and ch166 (index entry). No implementation or dedicated section. Critical for number-theoretic counting, GCD-related problems.
Not mentioned anywhere. Key technique for XOR maximization problems, subset XOR counting. Common in competitive programming.
Only a brief mention in ch163 (probability generating functions, ~10 lines). No combinatorial generating functions, no FPS operations (inverse, sqrt, log, exp).
Not covered. Used to evaluate polynomial at any point given n+1 values. Combined with FPS/NTT for polynomial operations.
Only mentioned in ch27 (MST context) and ch28/ch96 (NP-approximation). No dedicated coverage of Hierholzer's algorithm for finding Euler paths/circuits.
Covered within ch107 (HLD and Centroid Decomposition Applications). However, the basic concept of finding tree centroid could be in the foundational tree chapters.
ch87 exists but is only 121 lines — mostly overview + suffix array alternative. No Ukkonen's construction. Could be expanded.
ch88 exists but is only 136 lines. Could include more applications (count palindromic substrings by length, longest palindromic factorization, etc.)

missingTopics.topic: Gaussian Elimination over Finite Fields (XOR systems)
missingTopics.reason: ch73 covers Gaussian elimination for real numbers. XOR-based Gaussian elimination (for linear basis, GF(2)) is a distinct and important variant.
Concept is used in Mo's algorithm (ch62) and mentioned in ch54/ch76/ch102, but no standalone chapter explaining the general technique of block decomposition for arrays.
Mentioned in ch60 table of contents but no dedicated section with implementation. Important for modular equation solving.

missingTopics.topic: Floor Sum / Sum of Divisors / Divisor Counting
missingTopics.reason: Not covered. Common in competitive programming (e.g., counting lattice points, divisor summatory function).

missingTopics.topic: Persistent Segment Tree (Expanded with more variants)
missingTopics.reason: Covered in ch57 (section 57.6) and ch75. Already adequate, but could mention merge sort tree, segment tree with vectors at nodes.
ch45 has 474 lines with solid coverage. Could add more applications (number of distinct substrings of each length, LCS of multiple strings).

polynomialAlgorithms.details: No FFT, NTT, polynomial multiplication, FPS operations, or Berlekamp-Massey. This is a major gap for competitive programming coverage.

advancedNumberTheory.details: ch60 covers basics well (CRT, Lucas, Miller-Rabin, Pollard Rho overview). Missing: Möbius function/inversion, discrete logarithm, floor sum, generating functions.

graphAlgorithms.details: SCC, bridges, articulation points covered. Missing: 2-SAT, Hungarian, Min-Cost Max-Flow, Euler path/circuit.

combinatorics.details: ch71 covers permutations, combinations, Catalan, inclusion-exclusion, derangements, Stirling, Bell. Missing: Burnside's lemma, generating functions.

linearAlgebra.details: ch73 covers matrix ops and Gaussian elimination. Missing: XOR-basis/GF(2) systems, matrix rank applications.

topics: String matching with wildcards via FFT

recommendedNewChapters.proposedTitle: Generating Functions and Formal Power Series
topics: FPS operations (add, multiply, inverse, sqrt, log, exp)
